title: Strictly causal stop-debt state using current and prior PitStop
summary: Add features describing whether a driver still owes a strategic or regulatory stop, using only current and past pit-stop and compound information.
rationale: PitNextLap is not only tyre-life physics. A driver may be more or less likely to stop depending on whether a useful or required stop has already happened and whether current compound history leaves a remaining stop obligation. A strictly causal stop-debt state can complement tyre-life and finish-feasibility features.
implementation_hint: Sort by Race_Year, Driver, and LapNumber. Add a strictly causal stop-debt block using prior cumulative PitStop shifted by one row, current PitStop, prior and current-counted slick compound diversity, stop debt before and after the current lap, laps per remaining stop debt, CanFinishButStillOwesStop, and CurrentPitRelievesStopDebt. Skip the block if PitStop is missing in test.
expected_effect: Medium potential ROC AUC improvement because the signal complements fresh-slick feasibility and remaining-lap feasibility. Confidence is medium if grouped validation confirms the effect.
risk: Current-lap PitStop is safe only if it is a real input feature available in the test data at prediction time. Do not use future rows, PitNextLap, or shifted target proxies. There is local-overfit risk without grouped validation.
